{
  "roadmap_5_days": [
    "For the next 5 sessions, set a hard broker alarm at 14:25 on any expiry date (Jun/Jul/Aug/Sep/Oct cycle). When the alarm fires, your single permitted action is to close any existing OTM calls below ₹0.50 with under 2 days to expiry at market. Zero new entries permitted after 14:30 on expiry dates. Log the capital you did NOT deploy in the expiry-lottery column of your trade sheet with the label 'Capital Protected' — this reframes the compulsion as a cost avoidance win, not a missed opportunity.",
    "Before entering any trade in ASHOKLEY, FORTIS, or DMART — the three confirmed regret-loop instruments — you must run a mandatory 2-hour timestamp check against your last exit in that symbol. Open your broker terminal, filter the day's trades for that symbol, find the last exit timestamp, and only proceed if 120 minutes have elapsed AND you have re-run the scoring model showing total_score above 78 with f_score=1. If either condition fails, the trade does not happen. Write 'ASHOKLEY/FORTIS/DMART — 2HR + RESCORE ONLY' on a physical sticky note on your monitor.",
    "For the next 5 sessions, before clicking any BUY button, open a pre-trade sizing calculator (even a basic spreadsheet) and input total_score first. Score 80-92 gets 100% of your designated trade capital for that session. Score 60-79 gets 50%. Score below 60: close the ticket. The option's current price is entered LAST, after quantity is already fixed by the score formula. This directly attacks the HDFCBANK pattern (t=-2.75, f=0, 1100 qty) where price cheapness drove size instead of conviction. Track whether you referenced score before price in your journal for each trade."
  ],
  "roadmap_30_days": [
    "Architect a formal account bifurcation within 30 days: open a second broker sub-account or use a separate capital pool labeled 'Surgeon Book' funded with 80% of total trading capital. The Surgeon Book has exactly one entry rule — total_score above 80, f_score=1, instruments restricted to the proven universe: EICHERMOT, INDIANB, MARICO, HDFCLIFE, ASHOKLEY, PAYTM, CHOLAFIN, POLYCAB. The remaining 20% sits in a 'Speculative Pool' with a hard monthly loss limit of ₹5,000. When the speculative pool hits ₹5,000 loss in a calendar month, it is frozen until the 1st of next month. This creates the behavioral firewall your split-identity operation currently lacks — the Gambler cannot cannibalize the Surgeon's compounding curve if the capital is physically separated.",
    "Build and backtest an explicit exit checklist for Surgeon-mode trades within the next 30 days using the EICHERMOT 8-day hold and BRITANNIA exits as the reference template. Identify the specific signal that triggered each successful exit: was it a t_score reversal below zero, a regime shift from Extreme Bullish, or a score deterioration below 75? Document the exit trigger for every closed Surgeon-book trade over the next 30 sessions. By day 30, you should have a written, testable exit rule with at least 3 confirmed examples. This converts your current intuitive exit skill (exit efficiency +58.71%) into a replicable system that does not degrade under drawdown stress.",
    "Implement a premium-writing expansion protocol for instruments already in your universe — CHOLAFIN, HDFCLIFE, BSE, POLYCAB — within the next 30 days. The rule: any instrument in your Surgeon Book with score above 85, Extreme Bullish regime, and a short-term IV spike above its 20-day average becomes a short-premium candidate. Set a position limit of 2 short-premium trades simultaneously, each sized at 30% of Surgeon Book per-trade capital. This leverages your confirmed short-side discipline (better score adherence on the short book than the long speculative book) and creates a natural income buffer against the 13.24% losing trades without introducing new instruments or unfamiliar risk structures."
  ],
  "one_move_to_change_everything": "Implement the score-to-size formula immediately and universally — total_score above 80 with f_score=1 gets full designated trade capital, everything else gets zero — because the simulation shows a 5% win rate improvement scenario (achievable purely by routing capital away from low-conviction entries toward existing high-score setups) projects +₹65,266, a 24.5% P&L uplift, which is 19x larger than the stop-loss tightening simulation (₹1,744) and 28x larger than the revenge trade elimination simulation (₹2,296). This single rule would have prevented the HDFCBANK 1100-qty disaster (t=-2.75, f=0) and the BPCL 3950-qty entry (t=-0.95) while simultaneously ensuring BSE CE at score 91.5 received its deserved full allocation instead of only 750 qty. The high-score Surgeon trades already exist and are already winning — the capital just is not reaching them because price cheapness and emotional state currently override score in the sizing decision.",
  "one_move_rationale": "The forensic data is unambiguous: the trader's highest-score trades (score 80-92, f_score=1) are producing the 86.76% win rate and ₹2,410 average win that generate the 15.56 profit factor. The catastrophic trades — HDFCBANK at t=-2.75 with 1100 qty, BPCL at t=-0.95 with 3950 qty, the 18 zero-price expiry entries — share one structural feature: capital was allocated based on option price cheapness or emotional urgency rather than score conviction. The score-to-size formula does not require new instruments, new strategies, or behavioral change beyond the trade entry moment — it requires a single mechanical checkpoint that the trader's own analytical system already provides. Every other intervention (the 2-hour re-entry rule, the expiry cutoff, the account bifurcation) reduces damage from the Gambler. Only the score-to-size formula actively amplifies the Surgeon's compounding, which is the asymmetric lever this specific trader's curve requires."
}
